ddls:
CREATE TABLE "libraries" (
  "library_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each library branch */,
  "library_name" TEXT /* Name of the library branch */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the library */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the library branch */,
  "opening_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the library */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* URL to the library's website */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Geographical latitude of the library */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Geographical longitude of the library */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("library_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "books" (
  "book_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each book */,
  "call_number" TEXT /* Call number for cataloging the book */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the book */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the book */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the book */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the book (e.g., Check Shelf, Checked Out, Due) */,
  "library_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the library where the book is located */,
  "isbn" TEXT /* International Standard Book Number */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher of the book */,
  "publication_year" INTEGER /* Year of publication */,
  "page_count" INTEGER /* Number of pages in the book */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Brief summary of the book */,
  "cover_image_url" TEXT /* URL to the book's cover image */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the genre of the book */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_books_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_books_library_id FOREIGN KEY ("library_id") REFERENCES libraries ("library_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patrons" (
  "patron_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patron */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the patron */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the patron */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the patron */,
  "library_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the library branch the patron is associated with */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Patron's date of birth */,
  "address" TEXT /* Patron's address */,
  "membership_date" TEXT /* Date when the patron joined the library */,
  "membership_status" TEXT /* Status of the membership (e.g., active, expired) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patron_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patrons_library_id FOREIGN KEY ("library_id") REFERENCES libraries ("library_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loans" (
  "loan_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loan transaction */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book being loaned */,
  "patron_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patron who borrowed the book */,
  "loan_date" TEXT /* Date when the book was loaned */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Date when the book is due */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date when the book was returned */,
  "renewal_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the loan has been renewed */,
  "fine_amount" TEXT /* Fine amount if the book is overdue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("loan_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_loans_patron_id FOREIGN KEY ("patron_id") REFERENCES patrons ("patron_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_loans_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "overdue_books" (
  "overdue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each overdue entry */,
  "loan_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the loan transaction that is overdue */,
  "overdue_days" INTEGER /* Number of days the book is overdue */,
  "fine_accrued" TEXT /* Total fine accrued for the overdue period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("overdue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_overdue_books_loan_id FOREIGN KEY ("loan_id") REFERENCES loans ("loan_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "parent_genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to a parent genre for nested genres */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "staff" (
  "staff_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each staff member */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the staff member */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the staff member */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the staff member (e.g., librarian, assistant) */,
  "library_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the library branch where the staff member works */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the staff member started working */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the staff member ended working (null if currently employed) */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role of the staff member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("staff_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_staff_library_id FOREIGN KEY ("library_id") REFERENCES libraries ("library_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_staff_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reservations" (
  "reservation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reservation */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book being reserved */,
  "patron_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patron who reserved the book */,
  "reservation_date" TEXT /* Date when the book was reserved */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the reservation (e.g., active, fulfilled) */,
  "pickup_deadline" TEXT /* Deadline by which the reserved book must be picked up */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reservation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reservations_patron_id FOREIGN KEY ("patron_id") REFERENCES patrons ("patron_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reservations_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, librarian, patron) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its permissions */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Detailed permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "patron_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patron receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., overdue, reservation ready) */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read by the patron */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_patron_id FOREIGN KEY ("patron_id") REFERENCES patrons ("patron_id")
)
